Military Mar 26, 2026

The Top 5 Highest Paid US Air Force Pilot Ranks & Their Average 2026 Salaries

Air Force pilots are paid based on their rank and years of service, although even the highest ranks typically earn less than commercial pilots.

Air Force pilots at senior ranks earn considerably less than commercial airline pilots despite leadership responsibilities, creating potential retention challenges.

See Simple Flying →

Drones Mar 26, 2026

DJI Avata 360 drone launch delayed in US

DJI’s 360° drone launch may already be underway globally, but US buyers could be stuck waiting. A newly updated banner on DJI’s official Amazon US storefront now points to March 30 at 8 a.m. EST as the release timing for its upcoming DJI Avata 360 drone, subtly overriding the March 26 worldwide...

DJI's US market launch delay for the Avata 360 suggests ongoing regulatory or supply chain constraints affecting consumer drone availability in America despite global rollout.

See DroneDJ →

Light Dynamix will host its second Discovery Event on Thursday 30th April 2026 at The

Light Dynamix's recurring Discovery Events signal growing commercialization of drone show technology as a distinct entertainment and marketing vertical.

Supersonic jets could revolutionary coast-to-coast travel in the US.

Regulatory approval pathways for supersonic aircraft are being expedited through legislative action, potentially enabling commercial operations within 3-5 years rather than the traditional decade-long certification process.

A Hot Start to Spring in the Southwest

Temperatures in several states climbed over 100 degrees Fahrenheit in March 2026, which in some places was the highest March temperature on record.

Record-breaking heat in the Southwest could strain aircraft performance and increase runway length requirements during peak spring travel season.

Drones Mar 26, 2026

DJI just nuked drone prices across the board

Something strange is happening to DJI drone prices right now. And if you’ve ever even briefly considered buying a drone, this is one of those moments where ignoring it might come back to haunt you. Because this isn’t a typical seasonal sale or a small round of discounts tucked away on a product...

DJI's aggressive pricing strategy signals intensifying competition in the commercial drone market, potentially reshaping buyer expectations and accelerating industry consolidation.

Safety Culture in Aviation SMS

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

Effective SMS implementation requires psychological safety that encourages voluntary hazard reporting, as organizational culture directly influences whether employees proactively identify risks or remain silent.

Military Mar 26, 2026

Why The World’s Most-Produced Fighter Jet May Finally Disappear In 2026

Why the last combat-worthy MiG-21s likely disappeared in 2025, with only on-paper rusting hulks in service in 2026.

The MiG-21's retirement marks the end of a 60+ year operational reign, forcing remaining operators to rapidly modernize their air forces or face critical capability gaps.

See Simple Flying →

La NASA ofrecerá cobertura en vivo de los eventos previos al lanzamiento, el...

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Ames Experts Available for Artemis II Flight Test Interviews

NASA’s Ames Research Center in Silicon Valley invites media to interview local subject matter experts on Friday, March 27 from 10 a.m. to 2 p.m. ahead of the agency sending astronauts around the Moon for the first time in more than 50 years with the Artemis II flight test. NASA teams are gearing up...

NASA Ames is positioning Silicon Valley expertise as a central communication hub for public understanding of the first crewed lunar return mission in over five decades.

The San Francisco Bay area is complicated airspace, and right in the middle of it is San Carlos Airport. San Carlos airport is surrounded by terrain, San Francisco International class bravo,...

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Sets Coverage for First Artemis Crewed Mission Around Moon

A variety of prelaunch, launch, and mission events for NASA’s Artemis II mission around the Moon will stream online. The agency is targeting no earlier than Wednesday, April 1, for the test flight during a two-hour window that opens at 6:24 p.m. EDT, with additional launch opportunities through...

NASA's April 2025 Artemis II lunar mission marks the resumption of human deep-space exploration after a 50-year hiatus, directly influencing aerospace industry supply chains and commercial lunar economy development.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Space Force weighs launch alternatives as Vulcan faces potential months-long grounding

Officials explore shifting missions, extending satellites already in orbit

Space Force contingency planning for Vulcan delays reveals the strategic vulnerability of relying on single-provider launch capacity for national security missions.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Delta Air Lines Continues Los Angeles Lead, Schedules More Flights To Florida This Winter

Delta is adding more Florida flying from LAX as its battle with United intensifies.

Delta's Florida capacity expansion from LAX signals aggressive market share capture in high-yield leisure routes where carrier competition for connecting traffic remains fierce.

Safety Mar 26, 2026

RNO Mustang 9 Departure

Pilots departing from runways 17 left and right at Reno-Tahoe International need to pay special attention to the Mustang 9 Departure. It is extremely important that the procedure on the Mustang 9...

The Mustang 9 Departure requires heightened pilot attention due to specific procedural complexities that differ from standard RNO departures, potentially affecting safety margins.

JetBlue Considers Selling Itself To A Major US Carrier, Weighs How Washington Will React

JetBlue's exploration of a potential sale to rival airlines has sparked intense speculation and antitrust scrutiny.

JetBlue's sale consideration signals that even major carriers struggle with profitability in current market conditions, while antitrust concerns could block consolidation regardless of financial logic.

NASA Sets Coverage for Artemis II Moon Mission

Editor’s note: NASA will continuously update this Artemis II briefings and mission events page throughout prelaunch, launch, and mission activities. NASA will provide live coverage of prelaunch, launch, and mission events for the agency’s upcoming Artemis II crewed test flight around the Moon....

NASA's extensive media coverage strategy for Artemis II signals the agency's commitment to public engagement and maintaining congressional support for sustained lunar exploration funding.

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Artemis Moon Tree Dedicated in Honor of Mary W. Jackson

On March 18, 2026, students, staff, and members of NASA’s Langley Research Center gathered at Mary W. Jackson Elementary School in Hampton to celebrate the dedication of a remarkable addition to the campus – an Artemis Moon Tree. Although formally dedicated on this day, the loblolly pine had...

NASA's symbolic Artemis Moon Tree dedication honors Jackson's pioneering aerospace legacy while inspiring the next generation of engineers at a facility bearing her name.

See NASA →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across all three domains—airlines, airports, and flights—offers immediate emissions reductions without waiting for new aircraft technology adoption.

See MDPI →

Regulatory Mar 25, 2026

US House passes bill to clear way for overland supersonic flights

The US House of Representatives passed legislation aimed at opening the skies to civil supersonic flight over land,…

Removing the 55-year overland supersonic ban could unlock a lucrative market for next-gen aircraft while requiring airlines to navigate new noise certification standards.

Eve flies eVTOL prototype for Brazilian president in high-profile test milestone

Eve Air Mobility flew its full-scale engineering prototype for Brazilian authorities, including President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva,…

Government validation from a nation's highest office signals potential for eVTOL regulatory approval and domestic market adoption in Latin America.

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Names Jennifer Lyons Acting Launch Services Program Manager

NASA has selected Jennifer Lyons as acting program manager for the agency’s Launch Services Program (LSP) based at Kennedy Space Center in Florida. In this role, Lyons will lead NASA’s acquisition and management of domestic commercial launch services for science and robotic exploration missions...

NASA's LSP leadership transition signals continued reliance on commercial launch providers for science missions, affecting competition dynamics among SpaceX, United Launch Alliance, and emerging providers.

See NASA →

BRINC Unveils Guardian, Launching the Next Era of Drone as First Responder

First responder drones with persistent connectivity and all-weather capability are shifting public safety from reactive to continuous situational awareness.

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Office of Space Commerce releases mission authorization proposal

The Office of Space Commerce has rolled out its proposal for a “light touch” approach to mission authorization, the latest attempt in an effort to regulate new commercial space applications.

A streamlined authorization framework could accelerate commercial space operations while establishing baseline safety and national security oversight, directly impacting aerospace supply chains and launch service providers.

Unified brand presentation signals Textron's strategy to cross-sell its diverse product portfolio across general aviation, business, and emerging electric segments to the same customer base.

Cirrus posts strong 2025 with 691 SR Series deliveries, 106 Vision Jets

Cirrus Aircraft reported it delivered 691 SR Series piston aircraft and a record 106 Vision Jets in 2025,…

Cirrus's record Vision Jet sales suggest growing market appetite for single-engine jets, signaling a structural shift in personal aircraft preferences away from piston-only fleets.

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Webb Captures Saturn in Infrared

Saturn’s icy rings glow in this infrared view from NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope released on March 25, 2026. Combining this image with another that NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope captured – also released on the same day – provides scientists with a richer, more layered understanding of the gas...

Webb's infrared Saturn imagery demonstrates how complementary space telescope datasets enhance planetary science analysis beyond visible-light observations.
